name: sendgrid-automation
description: Automate SendGrid email operations including sending emails, managing contacts/lists, sender identities, templates, and analytics via Rube MCP (Composio). Always search tools first for current schemas.
qualityReason: Specific MCP automation with clear scope
capabilities: Create and send marketing email campaigns (Single Sends) to contact lists
Manage contact lists: create, update, search, and delete contacts
Set up and verify sender identities for sending emails
Retrieve email statistics and filter message activity
Manage suppression groups and unsubscribe settings
Handle async contact operations with proper wait times
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 192 static findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted markdown tool names (e.g., SENDGRID_RETRIEVE_ALL_LISTS) as shell commands, and legitimate service URLs as network exfiltration. This is a documentation-only skill for SendGrid API operations via Rube MCP. No actual code execution or security risks present.
valueStatement: SendGrid requires complex API workflows for marketing campaigns, contact management, and analytics. This skill provides ready-to-use sequences for common SendGrid operations so you can automate email campaigns without researching API documentation.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server with SendGrid toolkit to be configured
Requires active SendGrid API key authentication
Contact import operations are async and require 10-30 second wait times
Some features require paid SendGrid add-ons (e.g., 30 Days Email Activity History)

useCases.title: Marketing Team Sends Newsletter Campaign
useCases.description: A marketing team member wants to send a monthly newsletter to their subscriber list. They need to create a campaign, select the recipient list, and verify the sender is authenticated.

useCases.title: Developer Automates Contact Sync
useCases.description: A developer building a CRM needs to sync contacts to SendGrid for email outreach. They need to create lists, add contacts in bulk, and handle async processing correctly.

useCases.title: Business Owner Reviews Email Performance
useCases.description: A business owner wants to check email delivery rates, open rates, and click rates for the past month. They need to retrieve and interpret email statistics.

promptTemplates.title: Send a simple marketing email
promptTemplates.prompt:
I need to send a marketing email to my subscribers. First, list my available contact lists. Then create a Single Send campaign named 'Weekly Update' with subject 'Your Weekly Update' and HTML content '

Hello!

This week we have exciting news...

'. Send it to my marketing list.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to send a basic marketing email campaign

promptTemplates.title: Add new contacts to a list
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new contact list called 'New Signups' and add these contacts: john@example.com (first name: John), jane@example.com (first name: Jane). Put them in the 'New Signups' list.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to create a list and add contacts

promptTemplates.title: Check email statistics
promptTemplates.prompt: Get my email statistics for the last 30 days. Show me delivery rate, open rate, and click rate.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to review email campaign performance

promptTemplates.title: Set up a new sender identity
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to set up a new sender for my marketing emails. Create a sender identity with from email marketing@mydomain.com, from name 'My Company Marketing', reply-to support@mydomain.com, nickname 'Marketing Sender', and address '123 Main St, San Francisco, CA, US'.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs to configure a new sender for email campaigns

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before starting
Wait 10-30 seconds after async contact operations before verifying results
Use suppression groups or custom unsubscribe URLs for CAN-SPAM compliance
antiPatterns: Do not try to schedule a send by setting send_at on CREATE_SINGLE_SEND (use Schedule endpoint)
Do not mix legacy Contact Database API with Marketing API endpoints
Do not assume contacts appear immediately after add operations (they are async)

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server by Composio that provides access to SendGrid and other tools. You configure it in your AI client to enable SendGrid automation.

faq.question: Do I need a SendGrid API key?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an active SendGrid API key. The skill walks through connecting via Rube MCP, which handles the authentication flow.

faq.question: Why are my contacts not showing up after I add them?
faq.answer: Contact operations are asynchronous in SendGrid. After adding contacts, wait 10-30 seconds before checking with GET_CONTACTS_BY_EMAILS.

faq.question: Can I send to my Gmail or Yahoo list?
faq.answer: Avoid using gmail.com or yahoo.com as from addresses due to strict DMARC policies. Use a custom domain instead.

faq.question: How do I schedule an email campaign?
faq.answer: Create the Single Send first, then use the Schedule endpoint to set the send time. Setting send_at on CREATE does not schedule the send.

faq.question: What rate limits apply?
faq.answer: SendGrid has rate limits including 429 responses when throttled. Bulk contact upsert is limited to 30,000 contacts or 6MB per request.
